bridge_contract.note: Method/path shapes only. This does not prove Bot deployment, ENV, bearer/HMAC credentials, runtime authorization, schema, payment, provider, job, or delivery readiness.

gaps.detail: Bot source mappings that do not have an observed Web App route or guarded compatibility surface.
Private bridge routes are owned by the separate bot bridge branch, never the browser-facing Web App. Current checkout contract status: STATIC_CONTRACT_MATCHED
Direction-specific one-time Telegram callback contract. Current checkout status: STATIC_CALLBACK_CONTRACT_PRESENT
Bot-only tables need read/proxy contracts; the Web App must not duplicate wallet or PayOS writers.
Static feature-token presence differs between bot and Web App; inspect feature-specific routes before enabling a surface.

notes: Every statically discovered command/callback/conversation is represented in this matrix.
COPIED_GUARDED is a real signed/guarded Web compatibility surface, not a provider, wallet, job, or output success claim.
MAPPED_TO_EXISTING_ROUTE only confirms a static Web route was found; it does not prove auth, wallet, provider, job, or output parity.
TELEGRAM_ONLY records are intentionally not made browser actions without a separate product/security decision.

telegram_link_callback_contract.note: This verifies source markers and a static callback HMAC material shape only. It does not prove Bot deployment, Railway environment equality, actual secret equality, DNS/TLS reachability, Telegram delivery, or a successful customer callback.
